content.claim: A tightening of school-readiness rules from January 2020, which restricted school-start deferral and required six-year-olds to enter school rather than remain in kindergarten, is cited in sources as one plausible non-exclusive explanation for part of the observed SNI growth, implying that policy changes in adjacent domains (school-entry age) can measurably affect SEN identification without intent to change SEN policy itself.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: This causal explanation is explicitly presented as contested and non-exclusive, not verified
No quantified impact assessment isolates the contribution of the 2020 rule change from other drivers of identification growth
Alternative hypotheses (improved diagnostic methods, genuine prevalence increase, increased referral sensitivity) are possible and unranked
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
